title: cissus verticillata var. micrantha
intro: Cissus verticillata var. micrantha, also known as the small-leaved grape ivy or wild grape, is a perennial climber native to tropical Africa, with heart-shaped leaves and purple or greenish flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in various herbal remedies due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Cissus verticillata var. micrantha are attributed to its ability to support bone health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, osteoporosis, and skin infections.
